runway.en: a long, level strip where aircraft take off and land

ex.en: The plane sped down the runway before takeoff.

runway.en: a narrow stage that models walk along during a fashion show

ex.en: The models walked confidently down the runway.

sacred.en: connected with religion or regarded as holy

ex.en: This is a sacred place for many religions.

sacred.cue: 매우 중요해 함부로 할 수 없는
sacred.en: so important that it must be respected and not violated

ex.en: Family time is sacred to her.
ex.ko: 그녀에게 가족과 함께하는 시간은 신성한 것이다.

ex.en: She couldn't hide her sadness after the news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 슬픔을 감출 수 없었다.

safari.en: a trip to see or hunt wild animals, especially in Africa

ex.en: They went on a safari in Kenya.

safeguard.en: protect something from harm or damage

ex.en: The law safeguards workers' rights.

safeguard.en: a measure taken to protect against danger or damage

ex.en: The company added safeguards to protect customer data.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 고객 데이터를 보호하기 위한 안전장치를 추가했다.

safely.en: in a way that does not cause harm or danger

ex.en: They arrived home safely.

safety.en: the state of being safe from harm or danger

ex.en: Wear a helmet for your safety.

salesman.en: a man whose job is to sell products

ex.en: The salesman showed us the new models.

salmon.en: a large fish with pink flesh that lives in the sea and swims up rivers to lay eggs

ex.en: We had grilled salmon for dinner.
She painted the wall a soft salmon color.
ex.ko: 그녀는 벽을 부드러운 연분홍 주황색으로 칠했다.

sand.en: a substance made of very small grains of rock, found on beaches and in deserts

ex.en: The children built a castle in the sand.

sand.en: rub a surface with sandpaper to make it smooth

ex.en: He sanded the wooden table before painting it.
ex.ko: 그는 페인트칠하기 전에 나무 탁자를 사포로 문질렀다.

sandal.en: a light shoe with straps that leaves most of the foot uncovered

ex.en: She wore sandals to the beach.

satellite.en: an object that orbits a planet, either natural (like the moon) or man-made

ex.en: The satellite sends signals back to Earth.

satellite.en: controlled by or dependent on a larger, more powerful country or city

ex.en: The town grew as a satellite of the capital.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 수도의 위성 도시로 성장했다.

satisfaction.en: the pleasant feeling you have when you get what you wanted or needed

ex.en: She smiled with satisfaction after finishing the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 프로젝트를 끝내고 만족스러운 미소를 지었다.

satisfied.en: pleased because you got what you wanted or needed

ex.en: He seemed satisfied with the results.

saucepan.en: a deep pan with a long handle, used for cooking on a stove

ex.en: She heated the milk in a saucepan.

saucer.en: a small round plate that a cup stands on

ex.en: He placed the teacup on its saucer.
ex.ko: 그는 찻잔을 받침 접시 위에 놓았다.

scan.en: look through something quickly to find important points or information

ex.en: She scanned the newspaper for job ads.
ex.ko: 그녀는 구인 광고를 찾으려고 신문을 훑어보았다.

scan.en: use a machine to copy a document or image, or to read a code, into a computer

ex.en: Please scan your ticket at the gate.

scan.en: a medical test that uses equipment to produce an image of the inside of the body

ex.en: The doctor ordered a brain scan.
The loud noise scared the cat.
ex.ko: 그 큰 소리가 고양이를 놀라게 했다.

scare.en: a sudden feeling of fear, or an event that causes widespread worry

ex.en: There was a health scare at the school last week.
ex.ko: 지난주 학교에서 건강 문제로 소동이 있었다.

ex.en: She was scared of the dark as a child.
That was a really scary movie.

scatter.en: (of people or animals) move away in different directions

ex.en: The birds scattered when the dog barked.

scatter.en: throw or drop things so that they spread over an area

ex.en: She scattered flower petals on the table.
We took the scenic route along the coast.
ex.ko: 우리는 해안을 따라 경치 좋은 길로 갔다.

scholar.en: a person who studies a subject deeply and has great knowledge of it

ex.en: She is a well-known scholar of ancient history.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고대 역사 분야의 유명한 학자이다.

scholarship.en: money given to a student to help pay for their education, usually based on merit

ex.en: She won a scholarship to study abroad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 해외 유학을 위한 장학금을 받았다.

scholarship.en: serious academic study and knowledge in a particular subject

ex.en: The book reflects years of careful scholarship.
ex.ko: 그 책은 수년간의 신중한 학문적 연구를 반영한다.

schoolmate.en: someone who goes to school with you

ex.en: I ran into an old schoolmate at the mall.
ex.ko: 나는 쇼핑몰에서 옛 학교 친구를 우연히 만났다.

schoolteacher.en: a person whose job is teaching in a school

ex.en: Her mother works as a schoolteacher.

science fiction.en: stories about imaginary future science and technology, such as space travel or robots

ex.en: He loves reading science fiction novels.

scold.en: speak angrily to someone because they have done something wrong

ex.en: The teacher scolded him for being late.
ex.ko: 선생님은 그가 지각한 것에 대해 꾸짖었다.

scope.en: the range of things that a subject, activity, or plan deals with

ex.en: The report covers a wide scope of topics.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 넓은 범위의 주제를 다룬다.

score.en: get a point in a game or sport

ex.en: He scored the winning goal in the final minute.

score.en: the number of points a player or team has in a game or test

ex.en: What's the score of the game?

score.en: the number or grade you receive for a test or piece of work

ex.en: She got a perfect score on the exam.

score.en: the written form of a piece of music, especially for a film

ex.en: The composer worked on the film score for months.
ex.ko: 그 작곡가는 몇 달 동안 영화 음악을 작업했다.

scratch.en: rub your skin with your nails, especially because it itches

ex.en: He scratched his arm where the mosquito bit him.

scratch.en: make a mark on a surface with something sharp

ex.en: The cat scratched the sofa.

scratch.en: a small cut or mark made by scratching

ex.en: There's a scratch on the car door.

sculpture.en: a work of art made by carving or shaping stone, wood, or another material

ex.en: The museum displays ancient Greek sculptures.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 고대 그리스 조각품을 전시한다.

season.en: one of the four periods of the year (spring, summer, autumn, winter)

ex.en: Autumn is my favorite season.

season.en: a period of the year associated with a particular activity

ex.en: Tourist season starts in June here.

season.en: add salt, pepper, or spices to food to improve its taste

ex.en: Season the soup with salt and pepper.
Seawater is too salty to drink.
ex.ko: 바닷물은 너무 짜서 마실 수 없다.

seaweed.en: a plant that grows in the sea

ex.en: Seaweed is a common ingredient in Korean cooking.
ex.ko: 해초는 한국 요리에서 흔히 쓰이는 재료이다.

second-hand.en: not new; previously owned or used by someone else

ex.en: She bought a second-hand car.

second-hand.cue: 직접이 아니라 남을 통해 전해 들어
second-hand.en: not directly, but through someone else

ex.en: I only heard the news second-hand.
ex.ko: 나는 그 소식을 다른 사람을 통해 간접적으로 들었을 뿐이다.

secondary school.en: a school for students older than elementary school age, typically ages 11 to 18

ex.en: She started secondary school at age twelve.

secretly.en: in a way that other people do not know about

ex.en: He secretly planned a surprise party for her.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀를 위해 몰래 깜짝 파티를 계획했다.

ex.en: Make sure the ladder is secure before you climb.

secure.en: confident and free from fear or doubt

ex.en: She feels secure in her relationship.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 관계에 대해 안정감을 느낀다.

secure.en: succeed in getting or achieving something

ex.en: He secured a place at a top university.
Secure the boat to the dock.

security.en: protection against danger, theft, or harm

ex.en: The company improved airport security.

security.en: the state of being free from financial worry

ex.en: He values job security over a high salary.
ex.ko: 그는 높은 연봉보다 고용 안정을 중시한다.

seize.en: take hold of something suddenly and firmly

ex.en: She seized his arm to stop him from falling.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 넘어지지 않도록 그의 팔을 붙잡았다.

seize.en: take control of something by force

ex.en: Rebels seized the government building.

seize.en: take an opportunity eagerly and decisively

ex.en: She seized the chance to travel abroad.

select.en: choose someone or something from a group

ex.en: Please select your favorite color.

select.en: of very high quality, chosen carefully

ex.en: The store sells a select range of wines.

selection.en: the act of choosing someone or something

ex.en: The selection of the winner took a long time.
ex.ko: 우승자를 선정하는 데 오랜 시간이 걸렸다.

selection.cue: 고를 수 있는 여러 종류의 모음
selection.en: a range of different things that someone can choose from

ex.en: The shop has a wide selection of cheeses.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 다양한 종류의 치즈를 갖추고 있다.

self-service.en: describing a shop or restaurant where customers serve themselves

ex.en: It's a self-service restaurant, so get your own drinks.
ex.ko: 이곳은 셀프서비스 식당이라 음료는 직접 가져와야 한다.

selfish.en: caring only about yourself and not about other people

ex.en: It was selfish of him not to share.

sensation.en: a feeling in your body caused by something touching or affecting you

ex.en: She felt a strange sensation in her leg.

sensation.en: something or someone that causes great public excitement and interest

ex.en: The young singer became an overnight sensation.
ex.ko: 그 젊은 가수는 하룻밤 사이에 큰 화제가 되었다.

separation.en: the act or state of being apart or divided

ex.en: There should be a clear separation between work and personal life.
ex.ko: 일과 개인 생활 사이에는 명확한 분리가 있어야 한다.

separation.en: a situation in which a married couple lives apart

ex.en: They have been living in separation for a year.

sequence.en: the order in which things happen or are arranged

ex.en: Follow the correct sequence of steps.

sequence.en: a series of related shots or events in a film

ex.en: The chase sequence was thrilling to watch.
ex.ko: 그 추격 장면은 손에 땀을 쥐게 했다.

series.en: a number of similar things or events coming one after another

ex.en: There has been a series of accidents on this road.

series.en: a set of television or radio programs with the same characters or subject

ex.en: We binge-watched the whole series in a weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 주말 동안 그 시리즈 전체를 몰아봤다.

serious.en: not joking; meaning what you say

ex.en: Are you serious about quitting your job?

serious.en: very bad or dangerous, causing worry

ex.en: She suffered serious injuries in the accident.

serious.en: not playful or humorous in manner

ex.en: He always looks so serious.

server.en: a person who serves food and drinks in a restaurant

ex.en: Our server recommended the pasta.

server.en: a computer that provides data or services to other computers on a network

ex.en: The website went down because the server crashed.

service.en: the way that a business helps and looks after its customers

ex.en: The service at this restaurant is excellent.

service.en: a system or organization that provides something the public needs

ex.en: The bus service runs every ten minutes.

service.en: a religious ceremony, especially in a church

ex.en: They attend a service every Sunday morning.
ex.ko: 그들은 매주 일요일 아침 예배에 참석한다.

service.en: examine and repair a machine or vehicle

ex.en: I need to get my car serviced.

session.en: a period of time spent doing a particular activity

ex.en: We had a two-hour training session.
ex.ko: 우리는 두 시간짜리 교육 시간을 가졌다.

session.en: a formal meeting or period during which a group meets or a school holds classes

ex.en: Parliament is currently in session.

setting.en: the place and time in which a story or event takes place

ex.en: The novel's setting is 19th-century London.

setting.en: the surroundings or environment where something happens

ex.en: The hotel is in a beautiful mountain setting.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 아름다운 산속 환경에 자리하고 있다.

setting.en: the level or position that a machine or device is adjusted to

ex.en: Turn the oven to a low setting.

settle.en: make a place your permanent home

ex.en: They settled in Canada after the war.

settle.en: reach an agreement or come to a solution about a problem or argument

ex.en: They finally settled the dispute peacefully.
ex.ko: 그들은 마침내 그 분쟁을 평화적으로 해결했다.

settle.en: become calm or stop moving after being active or unsettled

ex.en: Wait for the dust to settle.

settlement.en: a place where people establish a new community to live

ex.en: The settlers built a small settlement near the river.
ex.ko: 정착민들은 강 근처에 작은 정착지를 세웠다.

settlement.en: an official agreement that ends an argument or disagreement

ex.en: The two companies reached a settlement out of court.
ex.ko: 두 회사는 법정 밖에서 합의에 도달했다.

settler.en: a person who moves to and settles in a new country or area

ex.en: Early settlers faced many hardships.
The region suffered severe drought last summer.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 지난여름 극심한 가뭄을 겪었다.

severe.en: strict and harsh in attitude or discipline

ex.en: The coach has a severe training regimen.
ex.ko: 그 코치는 엄격한 훈련 방식을 가지고 있다.

severely.en: to a very serious or intense degree

ex.en: He was severely injured in the crash.
ex.ko: 그는 그 충돌 사고로 심하게 다쳤다.

severely.en: in a strict or harsh manner

ex.en: She was punished severely for the mistake.

sex.en: the state of being male or female

ex.en: Please state your sex on the form.

shadowy.en: full of shadows or only dimly lit

ex.en: We walked through a shadowy alley.

shadowy.en: mysterious and not fully known or understood

ex.en: He has a shadowy past that no one talks about.
ex.ko: 그는 아무도 말하지 않는 모호한 과거를 가지고 있다.

shallow.en: not deep; having little distance from top to bottom

ex.en: Children can swim in the shallow end of the pool.
ex.ko: 아이들은 수영장의 얕은 쪽에서 수영할 수 있다.

shallow.en: showing little serious thought or knowledge

ex.en: I found the conversation rather shallow.
ex.ko: 나는 그 대화가 다소 피상적이라고 느꼈다.

shame.en: a painful feeling caused by knowing you have done something wrong or foolish

ex.en: He felt shame after lying to his friend.
ex.ko: 그는 친구에게 거짓말한 것에 대해 부끄러움을 느꼈다.

ex.en: It's a shame you can't come to the party.

shameful.en: deserving to cause feelings of shame; morally wrong

ex.en: It was shameful how they treated the workers.

shave.en: remove hair from your face or body using a razor

ex.en: He shaves every morning before work.
ex.ko: 그는 출근 전 매일 아침 면도를 한다.

ex.en: He needs a shave before the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접 전에 면도를 해야 한다.

sheer.en: complete and total, used to emphasize something

ex.en: It was sheer luck that we found the place.
ex.ko: 우리가 그곳을 찾은 건 순전히 운이었다.

sheer.en: rising or falling very steeply, almost straight up or down

ex.en: The sheer cliff dropped straight into the sea.

sheer.en: (of fabric) so thin that light passes through it

ex.en: She wore a sheer white blouse.
ex.ko: 그녀는 속이 비치는 흰색 블라우스를 입었다.

sheet.en: a large piece of cloth used to cover a bed

ex.en: She changed the bed sheets on Sunday.

sheet.en: a piece of paper, especially one used for writing or printing

ex.en: Please write your answer on a separate sheet.

sheet.en: a broad, thin, flat piece of material such as metal or glass

ex.en: The roof is covered with a sheet of metal.
ex.ko: 그 지붕은 얇은 금속판으로 덮여 있다.

shelter.en: a place that protects you from bad weather or danger

ex.en: They took shelter from the storm under a bridge.

shelter.en: protect someone or something, or go somewhere to be protected from danger or weather

ex.en: The trees sheltered us from the rain.

sheriff.en: the chief law enforcement officer of a county in the US

ex.en: The sheriff arrested the suspect at dawn.

shift.en: a change in position, direction, or attitude

ex.en: There has been a shift in public opinion.

shift.en: a set period of time during which a group of workers is on duty

ex.en: She works the night shift at the hospital.
ex.ko: 그녀는 병원에서 야간 교대 근무를 한다.

shift.en: move or change from one position or state to another

ex.en: He shifted his weight from one foot to the other.
ex.ko: 그는 한쪽 발에서 다른 쪽 발로 체중을 옮겼다.

ex.en: She polished her shoes until they were shiny.

shiver.en: shake slightly because you are cold or frightened

ex.en: She shivered in the cold wind.

shiver.en: a slight shaking movement of the body caused by cold or fear

ex.en: A shiver ran down his spine.

shocked.en: feeling great surprise and upset because of something unpleasant

ex.en: We were shocked by the news of the accident.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 사고 소식에 충격을 받았다.

shocking.en: causing great surprise or shock, especially because unpleasant

ex.en: The film contains some shocking scenes.
ex.ko: 그 영화에는 충격적인 장면들이 담겨 있다.

shopper.en: a person who is shopping or who shops regularly

ex.en: The mall was crowded with shoppers before the holidays.

shortage.en: a situation in which there is not enough of something that is needed

ex.en: The country is facing a serious water shortage.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 심각한 물 부족을 겪고 있다.

shortly.en: in a short time from now; soon

ex.en: The manager will be with you shortly.

shrimp.en: a small shellfish with a long tail, often eaten as food

ex.en: We ordered grilled shrimp for the appetizer.
He missed work due to sickness.

sickness.en: a feeling of wanting to vomit

ex.en: She felt a wave of sickness during the boat ride.
ex.ko: 그녀는 배를 타는 동안 메스꺼움을 느꼈다.

sidewalk.en: a paved path for pedestrians at the side of a road

ex.en: Children were playing on the sidewalk.

sigh.en: let out a long, deep breath to show tiredness, relief, or sadness

ex.en: She sighed and closed her book.

sigh.en: the sound made when you sigh

ex.en: He let out a sigh of relief.

signal.en: a gesture, sound, or action that gives information or an instruction

ex.en: The referee gave the signal to start the game.

signal.en: make a gesture or sound to give information or an instruction

ex.en: He signaled for the waiter to bring the check.
ex.ko: 그는 웨이터에게 계산서를 가져오라고 신호를 보냈다.

signature.en: your name written in your own handwriting

ex.en: Please put your signature at the bottom of the form.

signature.en: a distinctive quality or style that identifies someone or something

ex.en: Spicy noodles are the restaurant's signature dish.
ex.ko: 매운 국수는 그 식당의 대표 메뉴이다.

significance.en: the importance or meaning of something

ex.en: Historians debate the significance of the event.
ex.ko: 역사학자들은 그 사건의 의의를 두고 논쟁한다.

signpost.en: a sign beside a road that shows directions or place names

ex.en: Follow the signposts to the town center.
The house was silent at night.

silent.en: not speaking; refusing to say anything

ex.en: He remained silent during the interview.

silent.en: (of a letter) written but not pronounced

ex.en: The 'k' in 'knife' is silent.

silk.en: a smooth, soft fabric made from fibers produced by silkworms

ex.en: She wore a beautiful silk dress.

similarity.en: the quality of being similar to something else

ex.en: There is a strong similarity between the two designs.
ex.ko: 그 두 디자인 사이에는 강한 유사성이 있다.

similarly.en: in a similar way to something already mentioned

ex.en: Similarly, the second experiment showed the same result.
ex.ko: 마찬가지로, 두 번째 실험도 같은 결과를 보여주었다.

simplify.en: make something easier to do or understand

ex.en: The new app simplifies the payment process.
ex.ko: 그 새 앱은 결제 과정을 단순화한다.

ex.en: The two events happened simultaneously.

skeleton.en: the structure of bones that supports the body of a person or animal

ex.en: The museum has a dinosaur skeleton on display.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 공룡 골격을 전시하고 있다.

skeleton.en: the basic supporting structure of something, such as a building or plan

ex.en: The steel skeleton of the new building is already up.
ex.ko: 새 건물의 철제 골조가 이미 세워졌다.

skin.en: the natural outer covering of a person's or animal's body

ex.en: She has very sensitive skin.

skin.en: the outer covering of a fruit or vegetable

ex.en: Don't forget to wash the potato skin before cooking.
ex.ko: 요리하기 전에 감자 껍질을 씻는 것을 잊지 마.

skyscraper.en: a very tall building in a city

ex.en: New York is famous for its skyscrapers.

slavery.en: the system in which people are owned by others and forced to work for them

ex.en: Slavery was abolished in the United States in 1865.

slavery.en: work done under extremely harsh conditions with little or no freedom or pay

ex.en: The report exposed modern slavery in the fishing industry.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 어업계의 현대판 노예 노동을 폭로했다.

sleeve.en: the part of a piece of clothing that covers the arm

ex.en: He rolled up his sleeves before starting work.
ex.ko: 그는 일을 시작하기 전에 소매를 걷어붙였다.

ex.en: There has been a slight improvement in his condition.
She has a slight build for a runner.

slightly.en: to a small degree; not very

ex.en: The room was slightly warmer than outside.
ex.ko: 그 방은 밖보다 약간 더 따뜻했다.

slip.en: slide accidentally and lose your balance

ex.en: She slipped on the icy pavement.

slip.en: move or put something quickly and quietly

ex.en: He slipped the note into her pocket.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 주머니에 몰래 쪽지를 넣었다.

slip.en: a small mistake, especially in speech or writing

ex.en: It was just a slip of the tongue.

slip.en: a small piece of paper, especially for writing a short note

ex.en: Write your name on this slip of paper.

slogan.en: a short, memorable phrase used by a group, business, or campaign

ex.en: The company came up with a catchy new slogan.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 기억하기 쉬운 새 슬로건을 만들었다.

slope.en: a surface that is higher at one end than the other

ex.en: They skied down the steep slope.

slope.en: be at an angle; go up or down gradually

ex.en: The garden slopes down towards the river.
ex.ko: 그 정원은 강 쪽으로 완만하게 기울어져 있다.

slot.en: a narrow opening for putting something into, such as a coin or a letter

ex.en: Put a coin in the slot to start the machine.

slot.en: a particular time or place in a schedule or system

ex.en: I booked a slot for 3 p.m.
He is trying to quit as a heavy smoker.
ex.ko: 그는 골초라서 담배를 끊으려고 노력 중이다.

sneeze.en: make a sudden, uncontrolled sound through your nose and mouth, often because of a cold

ex.en: She sneezed several times because of her allergies.
ex.ko: 그녀는 알레르기 때문에 여러 번 재채기를 했다.

sneeze.en: the act or sound of sneezing

ex.en: He let out a loud sneeze.

snowboard.en: a board used for sliding down snow-covered slopes while standing on it

ex.en: He bought a new snowboard for the winter season.
ex.ko: 그는 겨울 시즌을 위해 새 스노보드를 샀다.

snowboard.en: slide down a snowy slope while standing on a snowboard

ex.en: We went snowboarding in the Alps last winter.

snowstorm.en: a storm with heavy snow and strong winds

ex.en: The snowstorm forced the school to close.

soap opera.en: a television series that follows the daily lives and relationships of a group of characters

ex.en: My grandmother watches a soap opera every afternoon.
ex.ko: 우리 할머니는 매일 오후 드라마를 보신다.

social networking.en: the activity of using websites or apps to connect and communicate with other people

ex.en: She spends a lot of time on social networking sites.
ex.ko: 그녀는 소셜 네트워킹 사이트에서 많은 시간을 보낸다.

socially.en: in relation to society as a whole

ex.en: The policy has both socially and economically important effects.
ex.ko: 그 정책은 사회적으로도 경제적으로도 중요한 영향을 미친다.

socially.en: in situations where you meet and talk with other people

ex.en: He's shy, but he does well socially once he relaxes.
ex.ko: 그는 수줍음이 많지만 긴장을 풀면 사람들과 잘 어울린다.

ex.en: The oil stain soiled his white shirt.
ex.ko: 기름 얼룩이 그의 흰 셔츠를 더럽혔다.

soil.en: the top layer of earth in which plants grow

ex.en: This soil is rich and good for growing vegetables.
ex.ko: 이 흙은 비옥해서 채소를 기르기에 좋다.

solid.en: having a firm shape and structure, not liquid or gas

ex.en: Water becomes solid when it freezes.
They built a solid wooden table.

solid.en: reliable and based on good reasons or evidence

ex.en: The police have solid evidence against him.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그에 대한 확실한 증거를 가지고 있다.

somehow.en: in a way that is not known or certain

ex.en: We'll get there somehow.

somehow.en: for a reason that is not clear or known

ex.en: Somehow, I don't trust him.

someplace.en: in, at, or to some place that is not specified

ex.en: I think I left my keys someplace in the kitchen.
ex.ko: 부엌 어딘가에 열쇠를 두고 온 것 같다.

sometime.en: at an unspecified point in the future

ex.en: Let's have lunch together sometime next week.
ex.ko: 다음 주 언젠가 같이 점심 먹자.

sometimes.en: on some occasions, but not always

ex.en: Sometimes I walk to work instead of driving.
ex.ko: 나는 가끔 운전 대신 걸어서 출근한다.

soothe.en: make someone feel calmer or less upset or in pain

ex.en: She sang softly to soothe the crying baby.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우는 아기를 달래려고 부드럽게 노래를 불렀다.

sophomore.en: a student in the second year of high school or college in the US

ex.en: She is a sophomore at the University of Michigan.

sore.en: painful, especially when touched or used

ex.en: My legs are sore after the long hike.
He's still sore about losing the game.
ex.ko: 그는 경기에 진 것에 대해 아직 화가 나 있다.

sore.en: a painful, infected spot on the skin

ex.en: She has a cold sore on her lip.

sorrow.en: a feeling of deep sadness, especially caused by loss

ex.en: She expressed her sorrow at the funeral.

sort.en: arrange things into different groups according to type

ex.en: Please sort these files by date.

sort.en: a type or kind of person or thing

ex.en: What sort of music do you like?

soul.en: the spiritual part of a person, believed to continue after death

ex.en: They believe the soul lives on after death.
ex.ko: 그들은 영혼이 죽음 이후에도 계속된다고 믿는다.

soul.en: a person, especially when talking about their character

ex.en: There wasn't a soul on the street at midnight.
ex.ko: 자정에 거리에는 사람이 한 명도 없었다.

soul.en: a type of emotional popular music that developed from gospel and rhythm and blues

ex.en: He loves classic soul music from the 1960s.

sour.en: having a sharp, acidic taste like lemon or vinegar

ex.en: This yogurt tastes a bit sour.

sour.en: (of a relationship or situation) become unpleasant or unfriendly

ex.en: Their friendship soured after the argument.
ex.ko: 그들의 우정은 그 다툼 이후 틀어졌다.

southeast.en: the direction or region halfway between south and east

ex.en: The wind is blowing from the southeast.

southeast.en: situated in or facing the southeast

ex.en: Our house has a southeast-facing balcony.

southern.en: situated in, or belonging to, the south of a place

ex.en: They moved to a small town in southern Italy.
ex.ko: 그들은 이탈리아 남부의 작은 마을로 이사했다.

southwest.en: the direction or region halfway between south and west

ex.en: We drove southwest toward the coast.
ex.ko: 우리는 해안 쪽으로 남서쪽을 향해 운전했다.

southwest.en: situated in or facing the southwest

ex.en: The southwest region gets very little rain.
ex.ko: 그 남서쪽 지역은 비가 거의 오지 않는다.

souvenir.en: an object you buy or keep to remember a place, event, or trip

ex.en: She bought a magnet as a souvenir from Paris.

sparkle.en: a small bright flash of light or the quality of shining brightly

ex.en: The diamond ring had a beautiful sparkle.

sparkle.en: shine with small flashes of light

ex.en: The snow sparkled in the sunlight.

specialist.en: a person who has particular skill in or knowledge of a certain subject

ex.en: He is a specialist in marine biology.

specialist.en: a doctor who specializes in a particular area of medicine

ex.en: My doctor referred me to a heart specialist.
ex.ko: 내 담당의는 나를 심장 전문의에게 보냈다.

specialize.en: focus your studies or business on a particular subject or area

ex.en: This law firm specializes in family cases.
ex.ko: 이 법률 사무소는 가사 사건을 전문으로 한다.

specially.en: for a particular purpose; more than usual

ex.en: This cake was specially made for her birthday.
ex.ko: 이 케이크는 그녀의 생일을 위해 특별히 만들어졌다.

spectacular.cue: 보는 사람을 놀라게 할 만큼 웅장한
spectacular.en: extremely impressive or exciting to watch

ex.en: We watched a spectacular fireworks show.

spectator.en: a person who watches an event, especially a sports match

ex.en: Thousands of spectators filled the stadium.
ex.ko: 수천 명의 관중이 경기장을 가득 채웠다.

spelling.en: the way in which the letters of a word are correctly arranged

ex.en: English spelling can be tricky for learners.
ex.ko: 영어 철자법은 학습자에게 까다로울 수 있다.

sphere.en: a round, solid shape like a ball

ex.en: The earth is roughly a sphere.

sphere.en: a particular area of activity, influence, or interest

ex.en: Technology now affects every sphere of our lives.
ex.ko: 기술은 이제 우리 삶의 모든 영역에 영향을 미친다.

spice.en: a substance made from a plant, used to add flavor to food

ex.en: This curry has a lot of spices in it.
ex.ko: 이 카레에는 향신료가 많이 들어 있다.

spice.en: make something more interesting or exciting

ex.en: They added music to spice up the party.
ex.ko: 그들은 파티에 흥을 더하려고 음악을 틀었다.

spicy.en: having a strong, hot taste, often because of spices

ex.en: I love spicy food, especially Thai curry.
ex.ko: 나는 매운 음식을 좋아하는데, 특히 태국 카레를 좋아한다.

spider.en: a small creature with eight legs that spins webs to catch insects

ex.en: A spider was weaving its web in the corner.
ex.ko: 거미 한 마리가 구석에서 거미줄을 짜고 있었다.

spinach.en: a leafy green vegetable eaten as food

ex.en: She added spinach to the salad.

spiral.en: having a coiled, winding shape that spirals around a center

ex.en: They climbed the spiral staircase to the tower.
ex.ko: 그들은 탑으로 이어지는 나선형 계단을 올라갔다.

spiral.en: increase or decrease rapidly and uncontrollably

ex.en: The country's debt continued to spiral.
ex.ko: 그 나라의 부채는 계속해서 급격히 늘어났다.

spirit.en: the non-physical part of a person connected with feelings and character

ex.en: Her spirit remained strong despite the illness.

spirit.en: the attitude or feeling shared by a group of people

ex.en: The whole team showed great spirit after the loss.
ex.ko: 팀 전체가 패배 후에도 좋은 사기를 보여주었다.

ex.en: According to legend, spirits haunt the old castle.
ex.ko: 전설에 따르면 유령들이 그 오래된 성에 출몰한다고 한다.

spiritual.en: relating to the human spirit or soul rather than physical things

ex.en: She found the retreat to be a deeply spiritual experience.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 수련회가 매우 영적인 경험이었다고 느꼈다.

spoil.en: ruin or damage something, such as a plan, mood, or event

ex.en: The rain spoiled our picnic.

spoil.en: (of food) become bad and unfit to eat

ex.en: The milk will spoil if you don't refrigerate it.
ex.ko: 냉장 보관하지 않으면 우유가 상할 것이다.

spoil.en: give someone too much of what they want, especially so that they behave badly

ex.en: Grandparents love to spoil their grandchildren.
ex.ko: 조부모들은 손주들의 응석을 받아주는 것을 좋아한다.

sponge.en: a soft, light object full of small holes, used for washing or cleaning

ex.en: She wiped the table with a sponge.

sponge.en: clean or absorb liquid using a sponge

ex.en: He sponged the spilled juice off the counter.
ex.ko: 그는 조리대에 쏟아진 주스를 스펀지로 닦았다.

sponsor.en: provide money or support for an event, activity, or person

ex.en: The company sponsors a local youth soccer team.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 지역 청소년 축구팀을 후원한다.

sponsor.en: a person or organization that provides financial support for an event, activity, or person

ex.en: The marathon's main sponsor is a sportswear brand.
ex.ko: 그 마라톤의 주요 후원사는 스포츠웨어 브랜드이다.

sports.en: relating to sports or games involving physical activity

ex.en: He bought a new pair of sports shoes.

sportsmanship.en: fair and honest behavior when playing a sport or game

ex.en: He showed great sportsmanship by congratulating his opponent.
ex.ko: 그는 상대에게 축하 인사를 건네며 훌륭한 스포츠맨십을 보여주었다.

stable.en: not likely to change or fail; firm and steady

ex.en: The patient's condition is now stable.

stable.en: a building where horses are kept

ex.en: She rode her horse back to the stable.

stall.en: a small stand or table where goods are displayed and sold, especially at a market

ex.en: She bought fresh vegetables from a market stall.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시장 가판대에서 신선한 채소를 샀다.

stall.en: (of an engine or vehicle) stop running suddenly

ex.en: The car stalled at the traffic light.

stall.en: delay doing something or avoid giving a clear answer

ex.en: He kept stalling instead of giving a direct answer.
ex.ko: 그는 직접적인 답을 주지 않고 계속 시간을 끌었다.

standard.en: usual, normal, and widely accepted or used

ex.en: This is the standard size for envelopes.

standard.en: a level of quality or achievement used as a basis for comparison

ex.en: The school has very high academic standards.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 학업 기준이 매우 높다.

stare.en: look at someone or something for a long time without moving your eyes

ex.en: She stared out the window, lost in thought.
ex.ko: 그녀는 생각에 잠긴 채 창밖을 응시했다.

stare.en: a long, fixed look at someone or something

ex.en: He gave her a puzzled stare.
